steps.title: Fastest path: identify the relevant dimensions first
steps.content: Count the relevant faces, dimensions, or scale factor before setting up equations. Lengths scale by $k$, areas by $k^2$, and volumes by $k^3$; for boxes and prisms, write the needed face or volume formula directly.

steps.title: Find the radius from the circumference
Calculate the cylinder volume
steps.content:
With $r = 5$ and $h = 13$:
$$V = \pi(5)^2(13) = 325\pi \text{ cubic meters}$$
The radius is squared because each circular base has area $\pi r^2$, and the height scales that area through the full cylinder. That confirms C is correct.
